compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
Leftover VCS conflict markers in committed source are unambiguous, polyglot, and zero-FP — deterministic
check territory, not an LLM pass. This is the #5 reclassification: a detector, not prompt text.

## What
Add a deterministic DET criterion (exec: DET) that flags VCS conflict markers (<<<<<<<, =======, >>>>>>>)
and obvious encoding corruption (NUL bytes). Advisory / fail-open (blocking_enabled false). Reuses the
Engine B detector consumer pattern (engine_b.scan -> apply_failclosed) and criteria_routing.json routing.
Granularity note (grounded from plan review): engine_b.scan + apply_failclosed operate whole-file with
diff-scoping applied as a post-filter on match LOCATION, not a native added-lines-only match. Reconcile
this: either (a) accept file-scoped match with a location post-filter to changed files (simplest), or
(b) add an added-line filter in the detector consumer. Decide and document; do NOT silently assume
added-lines-only. Drops the fuzzy dso parts (truncation stubs, pasted-non-code-text) as too FP-prone.

## Scope
A builtin detector rule + criteria_routing.json entry (SHARED — coordinate with C, E); detectors.py
wiring (and the added-line-scoping decision).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Conflict markers in changed files produce an advisory finding; clean diffs do not.
- [ ] The match-granularity decision (whole-file+location-filter vs added-line) is implemented + documented.
- [ ] Detector is polyglot and fail-open; no new BLOCK source.
- [ ] Recall+no-fire eval passes (marker present vs a legitimate '=======' inside a string/heredoc).

file_impact.reason: conflict-marker DET entry (shared-file: coordinate with C,E)

compiled_state.title: Deterministic conflict-marker detector (DET criterion)
